data.title: Quantum-Si announces pricing of $50M registered direct offering of common stock; shares drop
data.description:
• Quantum-Si (NASDAQ:QSI) said on Friday that it has entered into securities purchase agreements with certain institutional investors for the purchase and sale of 15.6M shares of common stock at a purchase price of $3.20 per share, pursuant to a registered direct offering, resulting in gross proceeds of approximately $50 million.
  • The closing of the offering is expected to occur on or about January 6, 2025.
  • The company intends to use the net proceeds from the offering for working capital and general corporate purposes.
  • Quantum-Si (QSI) shares were down more than 12% in postmarket trading.

data.title: Wall Street shakes off recent weakness, snaps longest losing streak since April
data.description:
U.S. stocks on Friday surged, as Wall Street finally appeared to shake off the doldrums afflicting it this week. A dull end to 2024 and a dour start to 2025 derailed the Santa Claus rally, and the three major averages posted weekly losses of nearly 1% each.

The benchmark S&P 500 (SP500) gained +1.26% to settle at 5,942.47 points. The gauge snapped a five-day losing streak, its longest such negative run since April.

Meanwhile, the blue-chip Dow (DJI) added +0.80% to conclude at 42,732.13 points, while the tech-heavy Nasdaq Composite (COMP:IND) increased +1.77% to finish at 19,621.68 points.

All 11 S&P sectors ended in the green, led by Consumer Discretionary and Technology.

Technology closed +1.6%, as every single member of the Magnificent 7 club climbed - except Apple (AAPL). Reuters earlier reported that sales of foreign-branded smartphones in China tumbled 47.4% Y/Y in November, citing government data. Apple (AAPL) is the largest foreign smartphone manufacturer in China.

Over in the fixed-income markets, U.S. Treasury yields were mixed. The 10-year yield (US10Y) was up 4 basis points to 4.60%, while the shorter-end 2-year yield (US2Y) was up 3 basis points to 4.29%.

For more, see how Treasury yields have done across the curve on the Seeking Alpha bond page.

Despite the first trading session of the new year ending in the red on Thursday, investors should take heart from the fact that the S&P 500's (SP500) performance on the first trading day of a year has historically been a poor barometer for eventual annual returns. See the chart here.

“One in the eye for Eeyores everywhere this week, as the equity markets put in a positive start to 2025. Uncertainty about the incoming Administration’s policy stances, and further uncertainty about how the market will react to actually implemented policy, is holding back animal spirits at present. We have an eye on the downside for sure, but our house view for now remains up. As always, we believe the best guide to price is, price, and price looks bullish to our eyes,” Alex King, investing group leader of Cestrian Capital Research, told Seeking Alpha.

The spotlight on Friday was captured by a collision between the financial world and politics. Shares of U. S. Steel (X) closed -6.5%, after U.S. President Joe Biden officially blocked the proposed $14.9B sale of the iconic 123-year-old American steel giant to Japan's Nippon Steel (OTCPK:NPSCY)(OTCPK:NISTF). The President cited national security concerns for his decision.

Beer and alcohol companies also saw some notable stock declines, after the U.S. Surgeon General urged for the addition of cancer warning labels to alcoholic drinks.

Turning to Friday's economic calendar, the Institute for Supply Management said economic activity in the U.S. manufacturing sector improved slightly in December from November, though still staying in contraction for the ninth straight month.

data.title: Goldman Sachs's Alex Golten appointed to chief risk officer
data.description:
Goldman Sachs (NYSE:GS) named Alex Golten chief risk officer of the investment bank, it said in a statement on Friday, adding that he also joined the firm's Management Committee, effective Jan. 1.

Golten has more than 27 years' experience at Goldman (NYSE:GS), having most recently served as head of Market Risk and Finance Risk. He has also been the firm’s chief credit risk officer, head of Enterprise Risk and co-head of Credit Risk for EMEA.

Succeeding Brian Lee, Golten leads the Risk Division and is responsible for setting the company's risk management standards. Lee became chairman of the Risk Division.

data.title: Wall Street analysts defend Carvana after short-seller report
data.description:
Carvana (NYSE:CVNA) shares remain under selling pressure and are down for a fifth consecutive day as Hindenburg’s short-seller report coupled with bearish technical indicators continues to weigh on the stock.

While Carvana (CVNA) shares were already defensive before Hindenburg’s harsh criticism of the company, the losses accelerated even as Wall Street analysts come out in defensive of the company. J.P. Morgan’s Rajat Gupta said that while there is room for improvement at the online used car retailer, his research “has not suggested any red flags,” and the current share price provided an opportunity to “buy on weakness.” Gupta maintained his Overweight rating on Carvana (CVNA).

BTIG’s Marvin Fong also found certain arguments presented by Hindenburg “unconvincing,” among those a claim that declining used car prices would translate into pressure on the company’s gross margin.

“We believe the better indicator of gross profit behavior is the spread of retail prices – what consumers are actually paying – to wholesale prices – what the used car retailer is paying to acquire vehicles,” Fong says. Using this argument, Carvana (CVNA) has realized “fatter” profits as this spread has widened.

BTIG’s Fong also addresses Hindenburg’s other arguments, including the relationship with Ally Financial (ALLY), the sale of $800M in receivables to an “unnamed party,” and prime delinquencies, all of which either contain flawed information, or are interpreted as negative when in fact could be considered a positive for Carvana (CVNA) when viewed more discriminately.

While shares have retreated by 22% over the past five days, breaching support at the 100-day moving average, the stock is up more than 300% from last year, outperforming the S&P 500 by more than 12x.

data.title: Boeing, DoJ seek to extend talks to revise plea deal over 737 MAX crashes
data.description:
Boeing (NYSE:BA) and prosecutors from the U.S. Justice Department told a court Friday they have not reached agreement on a revised plea deal stemming from two fatal 737 MAX crashes after a federal judge rejected an earlier deal because of a diversity and inclusion provision.

Boeing (BA) and the DoJ said in a joint filing they "continue to work in good faith" toward a new agreement, asking the U.S. District Judge to give them until February 16 to provide a new update.

The two sides are negotiating a new proposed plea deal to "include revisions to the proposed plea agreement that would address the reasons the Court rejected it," lawyers for DoJ's fraud section and the company told the judge.

Boeing (BA) agreed in July to plead guilty plea to a criminal fraud conspiracy charge related to the 737 MAX crashes and to pay a fine of up to $487M and spend $455M to improve safety and compliance practices over three years of court-supervised probation as part of the deal.

data.title: Bionano stock rallies 19% on ATM direct offering
data.description:
Bionano Genomics (NASDAQ:BNGO) stock rallied 19% Friday afternoon after the company announced a $10M registered direct offering priced at-the-market under Nasdaq rules.

Bionano (NASDAQ:BNGO) said it has entered into an agreement with certain institutional investors for the purchase and sale of 39.68M of its common shares and warrants to buy up to 39.68M common shares for a combined offering price of $0.252 per share and accompanying warrant in an offering priced at-the-market.

The warrants will have an exercise price of $0.252 per share. The closing is expected to occur on or about Jan. 6, according to a statement.

H.C. Wainwright is serving as exclusive placement agent on the deal.

data.title: Bitcoin ETF observes record $332M in daily outflows, but BTC holds strong above $98K
data.description:
Bitcoin (BTC-USD) investors closely watched a dramatic shift in the market on Thursday, as the world’s largest bitcoin-backed exchange-traded fund saw its biggest ever daily outflows, with more than $300M flowing out of the fund in a single day.

In total, the iShares Bitcoin Trust ETF (NASDAQ:IBIT) experienced a withdrawal of $332M which represents roughly 0.7% of the total assets of IBIT. Currently, IBIT holds $52.14B in AUM.

As of Friday afternoon, bitcoin is +1.7% and trades near $98.5K. The crypto is 9.7% below its record high of $108,364.

From a momentum perspective, bitcoin also trades north of its 20-, 50-, 100-, and 200-day moving average. It has climbed 8.1% from its earlier week’s low of $91.3K.

Moreover, IBIT sits at $56.25 a share, lower by 8.9% from its all-time high of $61.74.

For investors looking to monitor the crypto world, IBIT is not the only bitcoin backed ETF on the market. Other funds that are worth noting include: (ARKB), (GBTC), (BRRR), (BTCO), (HODL), (BTCW), (FBTC), (BITB), and (EZBC).

[Note: IBIT fund flow data is per Bloomberg.]

data.title: Catalyst Watch: CES, M&A votes and the Supreme Court tackles the TikTok case
data.description:
Welcome to Seeking Alpha's Catalyst Watch - a breakdown of some of next week's actionable events that stand out. Read more about the events that may impact stock prices next week.

Monday - January 6

  • Volatility watch - KULR Technology Group (KULR) has a high level of implied volatility based on options trading. CervoMed (CRVO) and Beyond Meat (BYND) are near the top of the list of stocks with the highest level of short interest outstanding on them. The most overbought stocks per their 14-day relative strength index include Pactiv Evergreen (PTVE), Kore Group (KORE), and Poseida (PSTX). The most oversold stocks per their 14-day Relative Strength Index include Innovative Industrial (IIPR), Martin Midstream Partners (MMLP), and Astrana Health Inc (ASTH)
  • All week - The Consumer Electronics Show will take place in Las Vegas from January 7 to January 10. Delta Air Lines (DAL) CEO Ed Bastian, Nvidia (NVDA) CEO Jensen Huang, Sirius XM (SIRI) CEO Jennifer Witz, X CEO Linda Yaccarino, Waymo (GOOG) Co-CEO Tekedra Mawakana, Accenture (ACN) CEO Julie Sweet, and AMD (AMD) CEO Lisa Su will be some of the notable keynote speakers. Companies hoping to make a splash at CES with their products include LG with TV introductions, Qualcomm (QCOM) with next-gen car technology, Gentex (GNTX) with its connected car products, Himax Technologies (HIMX) with new eye tracking technology, Abbott Laboraties (ABT) with new biowearable products, and SES AI (SES) with the reveal of a new AI-enhanced cell for robotics and drones. In the auto sector, Toyota Motor (TM), Honda Motor (HMC), and Faraday Future Intelligent (FFIE) are all expected to show off new prototype cars at CES. Analysts also have Qualcomm (QCOM) circled as a stock that may see some volatility based on the tech event.
  • All week - The Oil-Dri Corporation of America (ODC) 2-for-1 stock split will become effective.
  • All day - The IPO quiet period for Service Titan (TTAN) will expire to free up analysts to post ratings.
  • 10:00 a.m. The November Factory Orders report will be released.

Tuesday - January 7

  • Earnings watch - Notable companies due to report include Cal-Maine Foods (CALM) and RPM International (RPM).
  • All day - The IPO quiet period on Anteris Technologies Global (AVR) will expire to free up analysts to post ratings.
  • FDA watch - McDonald's (MCD) will launch its McValue platform in restaurants across the United States. The new value offering will include various features such as the $5 Meal Deal, exclusive in-app offers, local food and drink deals, and a new "Buy One, Add One for $1" option on popular menu items for breakfast, lunch, and dinner.

Wednesday - January 8

  • Earnings watch - Notable companies due to report include Jefferies Financial (JEF) and Albertsons Companies (ACI).
  • All day - The IPO lockup period expires for blocks of shares of Icon Energy (ICON).
  • 12:00 p.m. Seeking Alpha's Top Stocks 2025 Investing Forum will include high-conviction stock picks from Steven Cress, SA's VP of Quantitative Strategy, as well as Investing Group leaders J Mintzmyer (Value Investor’s Edge), Samuel Smith (High Yield Investor), and Alex King (Growth Investor Pro).
  • 2:00 p.m. The Federal Reserve will release the minutes from its last meeting.

Thursday - January 9

  • All day - The U.S. stock markets will be closed to honor former President Jimmy Carter.
  • M&A watch - Shareholders with Barnes Group Inc. (B) will meet to vote on the planned sale of the company to Apollo Funds. Shareholders with Consol Energy (CEIX) will vote on the company's planned merger with Arch Resources (ARCH).
  • Fed watch - Philadelphia Federal Reserve Bank President Patrick Harker, Richmond Federal Reserve Bank President Thomas Barkin, and Kansas City Federal Reserve Bank President Jeffrey Schmid are all scheduled to give talks.
  • 10:00 a.m. IMAX Corporation (IMAX) will host a fireside chat with IMAX China CEO Daniel Manwaring. The event will include the unveiling of IMAX China’s Chinese New Year slate, followed by a moderated discussion around the IMAX content portfolio and overall box office environment in China.
  • 1:00 p.m. Tesla (TSLA) CEO Elon Musk is scheduled to have a conversation with German politician Alice Weidel on social media site X.

Friday - January 10

  • Earnings watch - Notable companies due to report include Delta Air Lines (DAL).
  • All day - Meritage Homes (MTH) will begin trading on a split-adjusted basis following the company's 2-for-1 stock split.
  • All day - The Supreme Court will hear the TikTok case. The expedited timeline allows the court to consider the issue before the potential ban is set to take effect on January 19.
  • All day - Media Day at the Detroit Auto Show could include important updates from General Motors (GM), Ford Motor (F), Stellantis (STLA), Toyota (TM), and Volkswagen (OTCPK:VLKAF). Rivian Automotive (RIVN) plans to showcase the R2 and R3X models at the show.

data.title: Nissan Group reports total U.S. sales up 2.8% to 924,008 units in 2024
data.description:
• Nissan Group (OTCPK:NSANY) on Friday announced total calendar year 2024 U.S. sales of 924,008 units, a 2.8% Y/Y increase, compared to 898,796 units in the prior year.
  • The company reported Q4 2024 total U.S. sales of 222,484 units, a 10.3% increase, compared to 201,747 units in Q4 2023, mainly due to strong sales of Nissan models across the lineup, including the all-new Kicks, Versa and Pathfinder.
  • Nissan division reported total calendar year 2024 sales of 865,938, a year-over-year increase of 3.8%, compared to 834,097 units in 2023.
  • However, Infiniti Division, the luxury vehicle division of Nissan reported total calendar year 2024 sales of 58,070, a 10.2% Y/Y decline, compared to 64,699 units in 2023.
  • Source : Press Release

data.title: Bitcoin set to rise on weekly basis, stays below $100K
data.description:
Bitcoin (BTC-USD) is on course to rise 5% on a weekly basis, but stayed below the $100K mark.

The world's largest digital currency, which saw a robust performance in 2024, is expected to see another strong year.

However, the cryptocurrency seems to have had a rocky start. BTC dropped to $91.3K on Monday, days after Janet Yellen, secretary of the U.S. Treasury, warned Congress that the government is likely to reach its limit on borrowing as early as Jan. 14.

Bitcoin's (BTC-USD) price doesn't appear likely to recover soon, according to its history in relation to debt ceiling episodes, according to a media report.

According to a CoinDesk report, increasing the debt ceiling has historically led to Bitcoin dropping or underperforming in the past five instances.

In the week and until the time of writing, BTC stayed below the $100K threshold, and reached as high as $98.8K.

On Thursday, BlackRock’s IBIT, also recorded outflows of $332.6M, making it its largest ever, according to data from SoSoValue. The Nasdaq-listed IBIT is also the largest public fund tracking Bitcoin (BTC-USD).

"I advise against aggressively going long on Bitcoin at current prices; waiting for lower sentiment and prices may offer better buying opportunities," SA contributor Oriental Trader said.

The history of Bitcoin suggests that if an investor waits, the price will fall back into a lull at some point and when sentiment is low and negative, then it could be an interesting time to purchase, Oriental Trader added.

SA contributor, Frank Holmes, also advocated the need to have Bitcoin (BTC-USD) in the 60/40 portfolio.

"Despite its volatility, Bitcoin's market cap and growth potential make it a compelling addition alongside gold for wealth preservation and growth," Holmes said.

"Another sign of BTC heading towards being a mainstream asset class is its declining realized volatility, which would eventually add more diversification benefits and alpha to the traditional 60/40 portfolio. Volatility should continue to decline as an asset class matures, as our long-standing view is that crypto would be no different," said Augustine Fan, Head of Insights, SOFA.

More Notable News

Digihost Technology (DGHI) produced around 35 BTC in December, compared to 37 produced in the previous month.

Bitfarms (BITF) reported a month on month growth in the total BTC earned in December.

Do Kwon, the former CEO of Terraform Labs, which collapsed in 2022, was handed over on Tuesday to U.S. authorities by Montenegro, where he was arrested in March 2023.

Crypto-linked stocks were higher in premarket trade on Thursday, as traders hope 2025 to be a robust year for cryptocurrencies.

Bit Digital (BTBT) executed a master service agreement providing for 576 Nvidia H200 GPUs over a two-year term to a new client, an AI Compute Fund managed by DNA Holdings Venture, the company said.

MicroStrategy (MSTR) acquired 2,138 bitcoins (BTC-USD) for $209M in the past week, bringing its holdings to 446,400 bitcoins as of Dec. 29, 2024, which were bought for ~$27.9B, the company disclosed on Monday.

Bitcoin, Ether moves

Bitcoin (BTC-USD) was up 1.3% to $98.8K on Friday as at 2:29 pm. Ether (ETH-USD) rose 4.3% to $3.6K.
